mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in more than 55% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side constraints that disrupt manufacturing output and supply chains, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65% of periods following a decline in industrial production, invalidating the short-term supply shock transmission.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.5% year-over-year, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where CPI exceeds the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a resilient labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns, with negative outcomes occurring in over 50% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad economic strength that increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt contraction in manufacturing activity that forces immediate equity repricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Markets ignore the production drop and deliver positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of threshold-crossing events.
